<lean>
axiom Show : Type
axiom StrangerThings : Show
axiom BlackMirror : Show

axiom Netflix : Show → Prop
axiom Popular : Show → Prop
axiom Binge : Show → Prop        -- Karen binge-watches the show
axiom Download : Show → Prop     -- Karen downloads the show
axiom ShareWithLisa : Show → Prop

-- "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show.
axiom ST_netflix : Netflix StrangerThings
axiom ST_popular : Popular StrangerThings

-- If a Netflix show is popular, Karen will binge-watch it.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Show, Netflix x ∧ Popular x → Binge x

-- If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Show, Netflix x → (Binge x ↔ Download x)

-- Karen does not download "Black Mirror."
axiom BM_not_download : ¬Download BlackMirror

-- "Black Mirror" is a Netflix show.
axiom BM_netflix : Netflix BlackMirror

-- If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : Show, Netflix x → Binge x → ShareWithLisa x

-- Prove the negation of the statement: "Black Mirror is popular."
theorem black_mirror_not_popular : ¬Popular BlackMirror := by
  intro BM_popular
  have BM_binge : Binge BlackMirror := R1 BlackMirror (And.intro BM_netflix BM_popular)
  have hiff : Binge BlackMirror ↔ Download BlackMirror := R2 BlackMirror BM_netflix
  have hb_to_d : Binge BlackMirror → Download BlackMirror := Iff.mp hiff
  exact BM_not_download (hb_to_d BM_binge)
</lean>

ANSWER: False

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Entities
axiom Tom : obj
axiom Fluffy : obj
axiom OliveGarden : obj

-- Predicates
axiom Managed : obj → Prop
axiom AllowsPets : obj → Prop
axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom Cat : obj → Prop
axiom Rents : obj → obj → Prop
axiom Allowed : obj → obj → obj → Prop  -- Allowed person building pet

-- Facts from the context
axiom some_managed_allows_pets : ∃ b : obj, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b
axiom deposit_required_to_rent : ∀ p b, Managed b → Rents p b → True  -- modeled but unused
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x
axiom fluffy_is_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom olive_is_managed : Managed OliveGarden

-- Rent and deposit amounts
axiom Rent : obj → Nat
axiom Deposit : obj → Nat
axiom deposit_ge_rent : ∀ b, Managed b → Deposit b ≥ Rent b
axiom rent_olive_2000 : Rent OliveGarden = 2000
axiom gt_2000_1500 : 2000 > 1500

-- Rule: If a managed building allows pets, people are allowed to move in with a pet
axiom allow_rule : ∀ b, Managed b → AllowsPets b → ∀ p t, Pet t → Allowed p b t

-- Tom's conditional renting rule
axiom tom_will_rent_if_allowed_and_deposit_le_1500 :
  ∀ b, Managed b → Allowed Tom b Fluffy → Deposit b ≤ 1500 → Rents Tom b

-- A useful numerical consequence (not sufficient to decide "Allowed" at OliveGarden)
theorem deposit_olive_gt_1500 : 1500 < Deposit OliveGarden := by
  have h0 : Rent OliveGarden ≤ Deposit OliveGarden := deposit_ge_rent OliveGarden olive_is_managed
  have h1 : 2000 ≤ Deposit OliveGarden := by simpa [rent_olive_2000] using h0
  -- gt_2000_1500 is 2000 > 1500, i.e., 1500 < 2000
  have hlt : 1500 < 2000 := gt_2000_1500
  exact Nat.lt_of_lt_of_le hlt h1

-- Target statement: Allowed Tom OliveGarden Fluffy
-- We cannot derive ¬Allowed Tom OliveGarden Fluffy from the given axioms:
-- there is no information that OliveGarden allows or forbids pets.
-- The existence of some managed building that allows pets does not specify OliveGarden.
-- Hence we cannot prove the negation of the statement.

--   sorry
-- Cannot prove ¬Allowed Tom OliveGarden Fluffy from given axioms
</lean>

  cases disj with

  | inr h => exact h

axiom Spill : obj → Prop
axiom Tidy : obj → Prop
axiom CFG : obj → Prop  -- clumsy foodie who goes out frequently
axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop
axiom Value : obj → Prop  -- values order and spotlessness
axiom Family : obj → Prop -- family prioritizes order and spotlessness

-- Premises
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Spill x → ¬ Tidy x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, CFG x → Spill x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Value x → Cleanly x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Family x → Value x
axiom A6 : (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

theorem not_both : ¬ (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) := by
  intro h
  have hs : Spill Peter := And.left h
  have hc : Cleanly Peter := And.right h
  have ht : Tidy Peter := A3 Peter hc
  have hnt : ¬ Tidy Peter := A1 Peter hs
  exact hnt ht

theorem not_spill_not_clean : ¬ Spill Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
  cases A6 with
  | inl h =>
      exact False.elim (not_both h)
  | inr h =>
      exact h

theorem not_spill : ¬ Spill Peter := by
  have h := not_spill_not_clean
  exact h.left

theorem not_clean : ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
  have h := not_spill_not_clean
  exact h.right

theorem not_cfg : ¬ CFG Peter := by
  intro hcfg
  have hs : Spill Peter := A2 Peter hcfg
  exact not_spill hs

theorem not_family : ¬ Family Peter := by
  intro hfam
  have hv : Value Peter := A5 Peter hfam
  have hc : Cleanly Peter := A4 Peter hv
  exact not_clean hc

theorem peter_statement_false : ¬ (CFG Peter ∨ Family Peter) := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | inl hcfg => exact not_cfg hcfg
  | inr hfam => exact not_family hfam

axiom obj : Type

axiom Kelly : obj → Prop
axiom Style : obj → Prop

axiom Brutalist : obj → Prop
axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

-- All of Zaha Hadid's design styles that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Zaha x ∧ MaxAdores x → Interesting x
-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Brutalist x ∧ MaxAdores x → ¬Interesting x
-- Every style that Max adores is either Zaha Hadid's design style or Kelly Wearstler's design style.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Style x ∧ MaxAdores x → (Zaha x ∨ Kelly x)
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ MaxAdores x → Evocative x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Kelly x ∧ MaxAdores x → Dreamy x
-- If a design by Max that he adores has interesting geometries, then the design is a brutalist building and evocative.
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ MaxAdores x ∧ Interesting x → Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x

-- Target statement to negate would be: ∃ x, DesignByMax x ∧ Brutalist x
-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive ¬(∃ x, DesignByMax x ∧ Brutalist x).
-- There is no link forbidding a design by Max from being brutalist unless it is also adored and interesting.

-- An example derivable contradiction (not the target):
-- No object can be simultaneously a design by Max, a Zaha style, and adored by Max.
theorem no_design_by_max_zaha_adored : ∀ x : obj, DesignByMax x ∧ Zaha x ∧ MaxAdores x → False := by
  intro x hx
  have hDM : DesignByMax x := And.left hx
  have hZ : Zaha x := And.left (And.right hx)
  have hM : MaxAdores x := And.right (And.right hx)
  have hI : Interesting x := A1 x (And.intro hZ hM)
  have hBE : Brutalist x ∧ Evocative x := A6 x (And.intro hDM (And.intro hM hI))
  have hB : Brutalist x := And.left hBE
  have hNotI : ¬Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro hB hM)
  exact hNotI hI

-- However, this does not rule out the existence of a design by Max that is brutalist (without the Zaha/adored/interesting conditions).
-- Therefore we cannot prove the negation of the statement "A design by Max is a brutalist building."
</lean>

-- No brutalist buildings that Max adores have interesting geometries.

-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are evocative.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, (Kelly x ∧ Adores x) → Evocative x
-- All of Kelly Wearstler's design styles that Max adores are dreamy.

  have hA : Adores x := And.right hBA

    have hBr : Brutalist x := And.left hBrEv
    have hNotInt : ¬Interesting x := A2 x (And.intro hBr hA)
    have hFalse : False := hNotInt hInt
    exact False.elim hFalse
  | inr hK =>
    have hEv : Evocative x := A4 x (And.intro hK hA)
    have hDr : Dreamy x := A5 x (And.intro hK hA)
    exact And.intro hEv hDr

axiom ActivateLeg : obj → Prop

  exact Y0 ha

      exact False.elim hFalse

axiom Yuri_not_Activate : ¬Activate Yuri

  intro hPro
  have hJump : Jump Yuri := Pro_implies_Jump Yuri hPro
  have hLeap : Leap Yuri := Jump_implies_Leap Yuri hJump
  have hAct : Activate Yuri := Leap_implies_Activate Yuri hLeap
  exact Yuri_not_Activate hAct

  intro hImp
  have hNotPro : ¬Pro Yuri := not_Pro_Yuri
  have hAnte : ¬(American Yuri ∧ Pro Yuri) := by
    intro hAnd
    have hPro : Pro Yuri := And.right hAnd
    exact hNotPro hPro
  have hProFromImp : Pro Yuri := hImp hAnte
  exact hNotPro hProFromImp

-- All animals that are Peter's pets can breathe.

        have hBreath : CanBreathe Rock := T5 Rock (And.intro pR hAnimal)
        have f : False := hNotBreath hBreath
        exact False.elim f
      | inr hBird2 =>
        have hCanFly : CanFly Rock := T4 Rock (And.intro pR hBird2)
        exact T6 Rock (And.intro pR hCanFly)

axiom John : obj

axiom MakePlans : obj → Prop
axiom Engaged : obj → Prop
axiom InviteOthers : obj → Prop
axiom InviteFriends : obj → Prop
axiom WellAttended : obj → Prop
axiom LargerFamily : obj → Prop

-- All people who make wedding plans are engaged.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MakePlans x → Engaged x
-- All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, InviteOthers x → MakePlans x
-- Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, WellAttended x → InviteOthers x
-- Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
axiom A4 : ∃ x : obj, WellAttended x ∧ LargerFamily x
-- If John is engaged, then John does not (invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans).
axiom A5 : Engaged John → ¬(InviteFriends John ∧ MakePlans John)
-- If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
axiom A6 : LargerFamily John → (WellAttended John ∨ InviteFriends John)

-- Derived: well-attended implies engaged (for any person)
theorem wellAttended_implies_engaged (x : obj) : WellAttended x → Engaged x := by
  intro hwa
  have hio : InviteOthers x := A3 x hwa
  have hmp : MakePlans x := A2 x hio
  exact A1 x hmp

-- If John has a larger family and does not invite others, then John invites friends.
theorem LF_and_notIO_implies_IF : LargerFamily John → ¬InviteOthers John → InviteFriends John := by
  intro hLF hNotIO
  have hdisj : WellAttended John ∨ InviteFriends John := A6 hLF
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hWA =>
      have hIO : InviteOthers John := A3 John hWA
      exact False.elim (hNotIO hIO)
  | inr hIF =>
      exact hIF

-- Target statement to negate would be: (¬LargerFamily John) ∧ (¬InviteOthers John)
-- There is no way to derive a contradiction from assuming this conjunction with the given axioms.
-- Hence we cannot prove its negation from the premises.

axiom RequiresLabeled : obj → Prop

-- The ML algorithm for training the text summarization model requires labeled data
axiom Algo_requires_labels : RequiresLabeled Algo

theorem not_unsupervised_Algo : ¬Unsupervised Algo := by
  intro hU
  have hNoLab : ¬RequiresLabeled Algo := Unsupervised_no_labels Algo hU
  exact hNoLab Algo_requires_labels

  have hdisj := CategoryCoverage Algo Algo_is_ML
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hS => exact hS
  | inr hUR =>
    cases hUR with
    | inl hU =>
      exact False.elim (not_unsupervised_Algo hU)
    | inr hR =>
      exact False.elim (Algo_not_RL hR)

-- Cannot prove ¬Supervised Algo from the given axioms
</lean>

axiom A5 : ∀ a : Alg, UsedToTrainSOTA a → RequiresLabeledData a

-- Goal: prove that it is not the case that an unsupervised learning algorithm is used to train the SOTA model.
theorem no_unsupervised_used_for_SOTA : ¬(∃ a : Alg, UsedToTrainSOTA a ∧ Unsupervised a) := by

  have hReq : RequiresLabeledData a := A5 a haUsed

  exact hNoReq hReq

  intro _

axiom Calm : obj → Prop

-- All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Rabbit x ∧ Spotted x → Cute x
-- Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ Spotted x
-- The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Spotted x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)
-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Squirrel x ∧ Spotted x → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Spotted Rockie
axiom A7 : Calm Rockie

-- From Rockie being calm and spotted, Rockie cannot be a squirrel.
theorem not_squirrel_Rockie : ¬Squirrel Rockie := by
  intro hSq
  have hpair : Squirrel Rockie ∧ Spotted Rockie := And.intro hSq A6
  have hSk : Skittish Rockie := A5 Rockie hpair
  have hNotCalm : ¬Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk
  exact hNotCalm A7

-- From "only rabbits or squirrels are spotted" and not_squirrel, Rockie is a rabbit.
theorem rabbit_Rockie : Rabbit Rockie := by
  have h := A3 Rockie A6
  cases h with
  | inl hR => exact hR
  | inr hS =>
    have hFalse : False := not_squirrel_Rockie hS
    exact False.elim hFalse

-- Cannot prove ¬Turtle Rockie from the given axioms.
-- theorems about Turtle Rockie are independent of the above premises.
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Rabbit : obj → Prop
axiom Squirrel : obj → Prop
axiom Turtle : obj → Prop
axiom Near : obj → Prop
axiom Cute : obj → Prop
axiom Skittish : obj → Prop
axiom Calm : obj → Prop
axiom Rockie : obj

-- All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, (Rabbit x ∧ Near x) → Cute x
-- Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ Near x
-- The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Near x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)
-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Squirrel x ∧ Near x) → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Near Rockie
axiom A7 : Calm Rockie

-- From calm Rockie and "near-campus squirrels are skittish" and "skittish → ¬calm",
-- we deduce Rockie is not a squirrel.
theorem not_squirrel_Rockie : ¬ Squirrel Rockie := by
  intro hS
  have hSk : Skittish Rockie := A5 Rockie (And.intro hS A6)
  have hNotCalm : ¬ Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSk
  exact hNotCalm A7

-- Using "only near-campus animals are rabbits or squirrels" and the above,
-- Rockie must be a rabbit.
theorem rabbit_Rockie : Rabbit Rockie := by
  have h : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := A3 Rockie A6
  cases h with
  | inl hr => exact hr
  | inr hs => exact False.elim (not_squirrel_Rockie hs)

axiom obj : Type

-- Entities

axiom Fluffy : obj
axiom OliveGarden : obj

axiom AllowsPets : obj → Prop
axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom Cat : obj → Prop
axiom AllowedMoveIn : obj → obj → obj → Prop -- person → pet → building
axiom WillRent : obj → obj → Prop            -- person → building
axiom MonthlyRent : obj → Nat
axiom SecurityDeposit : obj → Nat

-- Context axioms
-- Pets are allowed in some managed buildings.
axiom some_managed_allows_pets : ∃ b : obj, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b

-- A deposit is required to rent an apartment in a managed building.
-- (We model the deposit amount function; requirement implicit.)

-- The security deposit is at least the monthly rent at a managed building.
axiom deposit_at_least_rent : ∀ b : obj, Managed b → MonthlyRent b ≤ SecurityDeposit b

-- Fluffy is Tom's cat.
axiom Fluffy_cat : Cat Fluffy

-- Cats are pets.
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x

-- The Olive Garden is a managed building.
axiom OG_managed : Managed OliveGarden

-- The monthly rent at the Olive Garden is $2000.
axiom OG_rent_2000 : MonthlyRent OliveGarden = 2000

-- $2000 is more than $1500.
axiom two_thousand_gt_1500 : 2000 > 1500

-- Tom will rent an apartment in a managed building if he is allowed to move in with Fluffy,
-- and the security deposit is no more than $1500.
axiom tom_rents_if_allowed_and_deposit_small :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → AllowedMoveIn Tom Fluffy b → SecurityDeposit b ≤ 1500 → WillRent Tom b

-- If a managed building allows pets, then people are allowed to move in with a pet.
axiom allows_pets_implies_allowed_move_in :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → AllowsPets b → ∀ person pet, Pet pet → AllowedMoveIn person pet b

-- Goal to negate: WillRent Tom OliveGarden
-- From the given axioms, we cannot derive ¬WillRent Tom OliveGarden.
-- The condition given for Tom to rent is sufficient, not necessary; thus we cannot conclude he won't rent otherwise.
-- Hence we cannot prove the negation of the statement.
</lean>

axiom obj : Type
axiom Rockie : obj

axiom Rabbit : obj → Prop
axiom Squirrel : obj → Prop
axiom Turtle : obj → Prop
axiom Spotted : obj → Prop
axiom Cute : obj → Prop
axiom Skittish : obj → Prop
axiom Calm : obj → Prop

-- All rabbits that can be spotted near the campus are cute.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, (Rabbit x ∧ Spotted x) → Cute x
-- Some turtles can be spotted near the campus.
axiom A2 : ∃ x : obj, Turtle x ∧ Spotted x
-- The only animals that can be spotted near the campus are rabbits and squirrels.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Spotted x → (Rabbit x ∨ Squirrel x)
-- If something is skittish, then it is not calm.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Skittish x → ¬Calm x
-- All the squirrels that can be spotted near the campus are skittish.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, (Squirrel x ∧ Spotted x) → Skittish x
-- Rockie can be spotted near the campus, and it is calm.
axiom A6 : Spotted Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie

-- We prove the negation of the statement:
-- If Rockie is cute and calm, then Rockie is a skittish turtle.
theorem negate_statement :
  ¬((Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie) → (Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie)) := by
  intro hImp
  have hCalm : Calm Rockie := And.right A6
  have hSpot : Spotted Rockie := And.left A6
  have notSk : ¬Skittish Rockie := by
    intro hsk
    have hnotCalm : ¬Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hsk
    exact hnotCalm hCalm
  have rabbitOrSquirrel : Rabbit Rockie ∨ Squirrel Rockie := A3 Rockie hSpot
  have hCute : Cute Rockie := by
    cases rabbitOrSquirrel with
    | inl hRabbit =>
        exact A1 Rockie (And.intro hRabbit hSpot)
    | inr hSquirrel =>
        have hSkittish : Skittish Rockie := A5 Rockie (And.intro hSquirrel hSpot)
        have hnotCalm : ¬Calm Rockie := A4 Rockie hSkittish
        exact False.elim (hnotCalm hCalm)
  have hAnte : Cute Rockie ∧ Calm Rockie := And.intro hCute hCalm
  have hConsequent : Skittish Rockie ∧ Turtle Rockie := hImp hAnte
  exact notSk (And.left hConsequent)
</lean>
